name: Homejoy
description: An on-demand home cleaning marketplace connecting customers with cleaners.
stated_failure_cause: Lawsuits were cited as a deciding factor, while reporting also points to retention, CAC, and marketplace economics.
symptoms: high CAC; weak repeat usage; worker classification lawsuits; funding difficulty
evidence_summary: Reporting indicates Homejoy struggled with retention and customer acquisition economics, while worker classification lawsuits made fundraising harder near shutdown.
notes: Good test case for multi-cause failure where public narrative differs from deeper operating issues.

name: Everpix
description: A consumer photo storage and organization app for automatically managing personal photo libraries.
stated_failure_cause: The company said it could not secure enough funding or find an acquirer to continue scaling the business.
symptoms: great product reputation; insufficient capital; weak monetization or scale
evidence_summary: Everpix had a well-regarded product but shut down after failing to secure sufficient funding or an acquisition, suggesting weak business scaling despite product quality.
notes: Need deeper source from The Verge if available for final dataset.

name: Wesabe
description: A personal finance management product that helped consumers track spending and manage money.
stated_failure_cause: Founder Marc Hedlund wrote that Wesabe lost to Mint because Mint solved the user problem more simply and effectively.
symptoms: strong competitor; product complexity; user experience disadvantage
evidence_summary: The founder's postmortem explains that Wesabe lost to Mint because Mint delivered a simpler experience that better matched consumer needs.
notes: Useful case for separating competition from product execution.

name: Turntable.fm
description: A social music platform where users joined rooms and collaboratively played music as virtual DJs.
stated_failure_cause: The company shut down the virtual DJ product to focus on Turntable Live after usage and momentum declined.
symptoms: hype decay; weak habit formation; music licensing constraints; declining usage
evidence_summary: Coverage and analysis suggest Turntable.fm failed to create durable user habits after early hype, while music licensing and product focus created additional pressure.
notes: Original product later had revival attempts, so dataset should clarify the 2013 shutdown of the virtual DJ product.

name: Secret
description: An anonymous social app for sharing posts with friends and nearby users.
stated_failure_cause: Founder David Byttow said Secret no longer represented the vision he had when starting the company.
symptoms: trust and safety issues; anonymous abuse; cofounder departure; declining fit with original vision
evidence_summary: Secret shut down after rapid rise and decline; founder statements point to the product diverging from the original vision, while external sources describe anonymity-related abuse and regulatory pressure.
notes: Founder postmortem is reflective but not a complete business breakdown.

name: Juicero
description: A connected consumer hardware startup selling a Wi-Fi-enabled juice press and proprietary juice packs.
stated_failure_cause: The company said continuing operations was no longer feasible after controversy around the product and its value proposition.
symptoms: expensive hardware; weak value proposition; bad press; proprietary consumables
evidence_summary: Juicero became a symbol of over-engineered hardware after reports showed juice packs could be squeezed by hand, undermining the need for the expensive device.
notes: Not early-stage in funding size, but useful for testing hardware and value-proposition categories.

name: Beepi
description: An online used-car marketplace that bought, inspected, and sold used cars.
stated_failure_cause: Beepi ran out of cash after failed sale attempts and inability to raise enough new funding.
symptoms: high burn; inventory risk; losses on cars; failed merger or sale; funding dependency
evidence_summary: Reporting indicates Beepi's operational model created large losses and burn, and the company shut down after financing and sale options failed.
notes: Large funding amount makes it less early-stage, but it is useful for marketplace operations stress-testing.

name: Atrium
description: A legaltech startup combining legal services for startups with software tools.
stated_failure_cause: Atrium shut down after earlier layoffs and a failed transition away from legal services toward software.
symptoms: services/software mismatch; layoffs; high burn; unclear scalable model
evidence_summary: Atrium raised heavily for a legal services/software model but shut down after the services business was not enough and the company could not convert the model into a scalable software business.
notes: Category is imperfect: legaltech services plus software. Good schema stress test.

name: Hakeema
description: A bootstrapped SaaS platform for social and public sector organizations.
stated_failure_cause: The founder said nonprofit customers needed custom platforms, turning the company into a profitable agency rather than a scalable SaaS.
symptoms: custom implementations; services-like work; profitable but not scalable
evidence_summary: The founder post says Hakeema had revenue and retention, but customer needs pushed the team toward custom agency work instead of a scalable software company.
notes: Strong indie/bootstrapped case with primary source.

name: Quibi
description: A mobile-first short-form video streaming service built for premium episodic content.
stated_failure_cause: The company shut down after failing to gain enough traction for its mobile-first paid video service.
symptoms: weak adoption; paid subscription resistance; COVID timing; strong streaming competition
evidence_summary: Quibi shut down after weak consumer adoption of its paid mobile-first video format, with timing and competition contributing to the failure.
notes: Not early-stage because of huge funding, included only as schema stress test; likely exclude from final MVP dataset or mark as non-core.
